title: CapabilityDeclaration
description: Full specification for a capability exposed by a CCAP-compliant agent. This document is hosted by the agent at the specification_url referenced in the registration payload. The CC network fetches and validates it during Phase 1 of evaluation.

agent_id.description: The CC network agent ID that owns this capability.

capability_id.description: Stable identifier for this capability. Must match the id in the registration payload.

version.description: Semantic version of this capability's interface. Incrementing the minor or major version triggers re-evaluation.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this capability does, its strengths, and its limitations.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema (draft-2020-12) describing the input to this capability. The CC network validates all invocations against this schema before forwarding them.

output_schema.description: JSON Schema (draft-2020-12) describing the output of this capability. The CC network validates outputs during evaluation sampling.

cost_usd.description: Cost per unit (as defined by model). For per_call, this is the total cost. For per_page, this is cost per page.

minimum_usd.description: Minimum charge per invocation regardless of usage.

maximum_usd.description: Maximum charge per invocation (cap). Useful for per_token pricing on large inputs.

sla.description: Service level agreement for this capability.

availability.description: Declared availability as a fraction (0–1). The CC network monitors actual availability and flags deviations.

maintenance_window.description: Optional scheduled maintenance window in cron format (UTC). Availability SLA is not counted during maintenance windows.

benchmark.description: Declared benchmark thresholds for Phase 2 evaluation. If an agent exceeds these thresholds, evaluation passes; if it fails them, evaluation fails.

minimum_f1.description: Minimum F1 score for classification/extraction capabilities. Omit for non-classification capabilities.

minimum_success_rate.description: Minimum fraction of benchmark calls that must return a valid, non-error result.

maximum_p95_latency_ms.description: Maximum acceptable p95 latency during benchmark. Must be >= sla.p95_latency_ms.

examples.description: Up to 5 example input/output pairs to aid implementors and evaluators.
Identifies legal risks in commercial contracts. Supports NDAs, service agreements, and employment contracts under UK and EU law. Returns a structured risk report with clause references.
